BBR-001
In Stock

Retatrutide

GLP/GIP/Glucagon Triple Agonist · 10 mg

Retatrutide (GLP-RT) is a tri-receptor agonist targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously. Research interest centers on its differential engagement of all three pathways compared to dual-agonist compounds, with ongoing clinical studies investigating metabolic and body composition endpoints.

Select Dosing →
BBR-004
BlendIn Stock

GLOW 70

GHK-Cu 70mg · TB-500 10mg · BPC-157 12mg · Total 70 mg

Enhanced-concentration formulation of the GLOW blend with doubled GHK-Cu and increased BPC-157 fractions. Designed for research protocols requiring higher peptide loading or extended study timelines. The GHK-Cu fraction has been independently studied for matrix metalloproteinase modulation and wound-healing signaling cascades.

BBR-005
In Stock

HCG

Human Chorionic Gonadotropin · 10,000 IU

Human chorionic gonadotropin is a glycoprotein hormone structurally similar to LH, binding the LH/CGR receptor. In research contexts it is used to study gonadotropin receptor signaling, Leydig cell function, and steroidogenesis pathways.

BBR-007
In Stock

Tesamorelin

GHRH Analog · 5 mg

Tesamorelin is a synthetic analog of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) that stimulates pituitary GH secretion. Research applications include investigation of GH pulsatility, IGF-1 axis modulation, and visceral adipose tissue metabolism.

Select Dosing →
BBR-008
In Stock

BPC-157

Body Protection Compound · 10 mg

BPC-157 is a 15-amino-acid partial sequence of body protection compound isolated from human gastric juice. Research has focused on its interactions with the nitric oxide system, angiogenesis, and growth factor receptor signaling. Extensively studied in preclinical models examining gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal tissue pathways.

BBR-009
In Stock

MOTS-c

Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide · 10 mg

MOTS-c (Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the 12S rRNA-c) is a mitochondria-encoded peptide with emerging research interest in metabolic signaling. Studies have investigated its role in AMPK pathway activation, glucose utilization, and age-related metabolic decline.

BBR-010
In Stock

Selank

Anxiolytic Heptapeptide · 5 mg

Selank is a synthetic analog of the endogenous tuftsin tetrapeptide. Research applications include investigation of GABAergic modulation, BDNF expression, and enkephalin metabolism. Studied for its interaction with serotonin and dopamine transport systems in preclinical neurological models.

BBR-011
In Stock

Semax

ACTH(4-7) Analog · 10 mg

Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide analog of ACTH(4-10), engineered for metabolic stability. It has been investigated for its influence on BDNF and NGF expression, monoamine neurotransmitter systems, and microcirculation.

BBR-012
In Stock

GHK-Cu

Copper Tripeptide · 100 mg

GHK-Cu (glycine-histidine-lysine copper complex) is a naturally occurring tripeptide with copper chelation properties. Research has examined its roles in collagen and elastin synthesis, matrix metalloproteinase regulation, antioxidant gene expression, and wound repair signaling.

BBR-013
Limited

Melanotan II

Melanocortin Receptor Agonist · 10 mg

Melanotan II (MT-II) is a synthetic analog of α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH), acting as a non-selective melanocortin receptor agonist. Research interest spans melanogenesis pathways, MC3R/MC4R signaling, and energy homeostasis.

BBR-022
In Stock

Somatropin

Recombinant Human Growth Hormone · 15 IU

Recombinant human growth hormone (rhGH) with identical 191-amino-acid sequence to endogenous somatropin. Used in research studying somatotropic axis function, IGF-1 signaling, lipolysis, and protein anabolism.

Select Dosing →
BBR-015
In Stock

NAD+

Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide · 500 mg

NAD+ is a critical coenzyme in cellular redox reactions and a substrate for sirtuins and PARPs. Research interest has expanded into its roles in mitochondrial biogenesis, DNA repair signaling, and age-related metabolic decline.

BBR-016
In Stock

Tirzepatide

GIP/GLP-1 Dual Agonist · 10 mg

Tirzepatide is a dual agonist targeting both GIP (glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide) and GLP-1 receptors via a single synthetic molecule. Research interest centers on its simultaneous engagement of two incretin pathways and the differential effects compared to mono-agonist compounds.

BBR-017
In Stock

CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin

GHRH Analog + Ghrelin Mimetic · 5 mg + 5 mg

A combination preparation of CJC-1295 without DAC (a GHRH analog) and Ipamorelin (a selective ghrelin receptor agonist). Research applications center on synergistic stimulation of pituitary GH secretion through complementary receptor mechanisms.

BBR-018
In Stock

Thymosin Alpha-1

Thymic Peptide · 10 mg

Thymosin Alpha-1 (Tα1) is a 28-amino-acid peptide derived from the thymic protein prothymosin alpha. Research interest focuses on its modulatory effects on dendritic cell maturation, T-cell differentiation, and innate immune signaling pathways including TLR-mediated responses.

BBR-019
BlendIn Stock

Wolverine

BPC-157 10mg · TB-500 10mg · Total 20 mg

A dual-peptide blend combining BPC-157 and TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4 fragment) in equal parts. BPC-157 has been studied for its interactions with the nitric oxide system and growth factor signaling, while TB-500 research has focused on actin-binding properties and angiogenic pathways.

BBR-020
In Stock

Pinealon

Peptide Bioregulator · 10 mg

Pinealon is a synthetic tripeptide (Glu-Asp-Arg) bioregulator. Research interest centers on its proposed influence on neuronal gene expression, antioxidant defense pathways, and cellular responses to oxidative and hypoxic stress in preclinical neurological models.

BBR-021
In Stock

Epitalon

Pineal Tetrapeptide · 10 mg

Epitalon (Epithalon) is a synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) modeled on the pineal peptide epithalamin. Research has examined its proposed role in telomerase activity, circadian and melatonin regulation, and age-related cellular signaling pathways.

Terms

Who We Are

Research-grade compounds, rigorously sourced

Vegas Peps supplies qualified researchers with a curated selection of peptide compounds for scientific investigation. Our catalog is maintained with attention to purity documentation and compound integrity.

All compounds listed on this platform are strictly for research purposes. We do not provide medical advice, facilitate human administration, or accept orders through this website.

ContactFor questions, concerns, or more information, reach out to Trevor directly at 702-374-1652.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to know about sourcing, delivery, and quality assurance. Tap a question to expand.

All reconstitution is done with pharmaceutical-grade Pfizer Hospira bacteriostatic water — no generic or unbranded diluents. This ensures consistency and sterility across every preparation.

Each kit includes careful, professional reconstitution with Pfizer Hospira bacteriostatic water, plus the supplies you need to get started. Syringe counts are tailored to how the compound is typically dosed:

  • Daily / near-daily peptidesUp to 30 syringes
  • Less frequent schedules4–5 weeks’ supply

For compounds commonly dosed daily, kits come with a maximum of 30 syringes. For everything else, I provide enough syringes and alcohol pads to cover roughly four to five weeks of a common injection schedule.

Kit pricing reflects dosing frequency: compounds that need only infrequent dosing are priced close to the standalone powder, while daily or near-daily peptides carry a larger gap between the kit and powder-only options.

Delivery pricing is based on drive time from South Point Casino and Resort:

  • Within 10 minutesFree
  • 10–20 minutes$5
  • 20+ minutes$10

Delivery fees may be adjusted by previous mutual agreement in certain cases.

Every compound arrives with a Certificate of Analysis (COA) provided by the supplier, documenting purity and identity. In addition, random independent testing is conducted personally to verify supplier results and maintain quality standards.

When you order, I can share common research schedules and handling references for what you’ve purchased. In general, a few practices apply across the board:

  • Storage: Keep all reconstituted peptides refrigerated. The fridge door is preferable to the back — the back runs colder and can partially freeze the vial.
  • Handling: No vigorous shaking. Be gentle with the vial; swirl rather than shake.
  • Sterile technique: Swab both the vial stopper and the injection site with a prep pad, and let both fully dry before drawing and injecting.

New to drawing and injecting? Watch this quick walkthrough for first-timers →

By purchasing, you acknowledge and agree to the following:

  • Research use only. All compounds are sold strictly for laboratory and research purposes. They are not for human or animal consumption and are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.
  • No medical advice. No medical, clinical, or professional advice is provided. Any research schedules or references shared are informational only and are not a substitute for guidance from a qualified professional.
  • Buyer responsibility. The buyer is solely responsible for the safe, legal, and appropriate handling, storage, and use of all compounds, and for compliance with all applicable local, state, and federal regulations.
  • Age. Buyers must be 18 years of age or older.
  • No returns on opened items. Due to the nature of these products, reconstituted or opened vials cannot be returned. Reach out directly with any issues and they will be addressed case by case.

These terms may be updated at any time. Questions can be directed to Trevor at 702-374-1652.
